task_completion.description: Identify the HEC-RAS 6.6 Linux compute engine from the system PATH
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal shows a command used to locate or verify the HEC-RAS executable in the PATH (e.g., 'which RasUnsteady64', 'find', 'ls' in bin directories, or auto-completing the engine name).

task_completion.description: Run the unsteady flow simulation command
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal shows the execution command invoking the HEC-RAS unsteady compute engine (e.g., 'RasUnsteady64') with the appropriate Muncie project or plan file as an argument.
Terminal displays standard output from the HEC-RAS engine showing active computational progress, such as time steps, iterations, or 2D flow calculations.

Terminal shows a completion message from the HEC-RAS engine (e.g., 'Finished Unsteady Flow Simulation', 'Computation Complete') or returns to the command prompt without fatal errors after the progress output.

task_completion.description: Rename the resulting output HDF5 file to Muncie.p04.hdf
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal shows a 'mv' command renaming an '.hdf' file to 'Muncie.p04.hdf', or a subsequent 'ls' command confirming 'Muncie.p04.hdf' exists in the project directory.

integrity.description: Agent must use the terminal, not graphical interfaces
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the simulation was run and the file was renamed entirely via terminal commands. The HEC-RAS GUI or a graphical file manager should not be used.

integrity.description: Agent actually ran the simulation and did not just create a dummy file
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the terminal shows genuine execution output of the HEC-RAS engine before the file renaming occurs. The agent should not simply use 'touch Muncie.p04.hdf' or rename a pre-existing file without running the simulation.

integrity.description: Agent did not spoof the simulation output
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the agent did not use 'echo', 'cat', or a simple python script to print fake simulation progress to the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The HEC-RAS Linux compute engine for unsteady flow is typically named 'RasUnsteady64' or similar. The simulation operates on the Muncie White River dataset. Running the simulation will generate an HDF5 output file (often named after the plan, e.g., Muncie.p01.hdf or similar) which the agent must explicitly rename to exactly 'Muncie.p04.hdf'.
